patterns.reminder: AUTH_SECRET assigned a literal value. Load from env at runtime; never inline. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`.
Upstash REST token inlined. Move to `.env.local` or Vercel env vars. Rotate immediately if this leaks.
Envio Hasura token inlined. Read from env at runtime; never commit.
Sentry auth token inlined. Read from env; never commit. Rotate via Sentry org auth tokens page if this leaks.
Dune API key inlined. Read from env; never commit. Rotate via dune.com → Settings → API.
Arkham API key inlined. Read from env; never commit. Rotate via intel.arkm.com → Settings if this leaks.
Slack bot token inlined in an env assignment. Read from env; never commit. Rotate via Slack app settings if this leaks.
Slack token literal detected. Move to env. Rotate via Slack app settings if this commits.
AWS access-key literal detected. Use workload identity / instance profiles, not literal credentials.

64-hex literal looks like a private key or signature. If it's a contract address it should be 40 hex (0x[40 chars]). Transaction and block hashes are also 64 hex chars — if that's the case, move the constant to config or a fixture. Confirm and move secrets to env.
Hasura `_aggregate` queries are unbounded on prod and have caused 429 storms. Use a pre-rolled snapshot entity or paginate + count client-side.
App code is shelling out to gcloud. Use the GCP client libraries with workload identity instead — CLI calls won't work in Vercel/Cloud Run.

Throwing from an Envio handler (any form — Error, TypeError, custom, re-throw) halts the indexer (Sev-1). Use `context.log.error('<area>.<event>', {...})` and return early so the next event is processed.

Coercing an on-chain bigint to Number loses precision above 2^53. Keep it as bigint, or format for display only at the very edge. Matches suffixed forms too (amount0In, reserve1, netflowGlobal, limit0, etc.).

Third-party Actions must be SHA-pinned, not tag- or branch-pinned. Use the full 40-char commit SHA followed by a `# tag` comment. Catches @v1, @v1.2.3, @main, @latest, @stable, and subdirectory actions (owner/repo/path@ref).
Workflow grants write permission. Confirm pull_request triggers gate on `github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository` so forks don't get write tokens.

QuickNode webhook handler reads request body — verify the signature with `verify-quicknode-signature.ts` BEFORE parsing the body, not after. Reject unauthenticated requests early. (check-payload-size.ts is the intentional pre-sig DoS-rejection gate and is excluded from this rule.)
